response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented generalist with real developer-tooling passion and even direct IDE experience (NI LabWindows/CVI), but the core requirements of this role are largely unmet: his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/full-stack work, with only incidental Java, no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and no demonstrated JVM internals depth. The IDE work was brief, in C, and over a decade old, making him a poor fit for enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE tooling despite strong general engineering ability.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience contributing C/C++ components to NI LabWindows/CVI
Strong developer-tooling and testing track record (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, TDD advocacy)
Proven work on high-performance, large-scale systems (low-latency services handling millions of requests per second)
concerns: Minimal Java experience and no Kotlin or JVM internals background, the role's central requirement
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise framework and application-server experience
IDE experience is short (about seven months), dated (2014-2015), and in C rather than the JVM ecosystem
